methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Stellaria is a seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Nicolas Breton and backed by CEA and Schneider Electric, having raised €23M to date. The nuclear energy sector contains a fragmented peer universe of 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Traditional peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to Stellaria. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 private peers total, but filtering for same-stage and adjacent-stage companies with comparable valuation tiers yields fewer than the three datapoints required for statistical reliability. Applying an all-sector median without stage and maturity controls would introduce material distortion. The public comparable set is similarly limited in size and stage relevance, given the early-stage nature of Stellaria relative to publicly traded peers.

Without sufficient narrow-set peers to support quantitative triangulation, valuation of Stellaria requires primary analysis of company-specific metrics and market conditions. Key uncertainties include the competitive positioning of Stellaria's technology within the crowded advanced reactor landscape, the trajectory of regulatory approval timelines, and capital intensity of commercialization. Any valuation conclusion would depend on non-peer-based factors: management track record, technology differentiation, and the sustainability of current funding availability in nuclear venture markets. Further data on technology milestones, IP strength, and management experience is needed to support a point estimate.
